summary: Tested new payroll period tracking feature. Backend: 98.6% pass (73/74 - 1 pre-existing flaky test). Frontend: All payroll features working correctly.

minor.issue: Pre-existing flaky test - created employee not found in list immediately after creation (timing issue)

issues: Console hydration warnings: '<tr> cannot be child of <span>' - React rendering warning in table rows. Non-blocking but should be addressed for cleaner console.

details: GET /api/admin/payroll/settings returns default or configured settings
PUT /api/admin/payroll/settings updates pay period start date and default hourly rate
Settings persist after update
Settings require admin access (403 for employees)
POST /api/admin/payroll/report generates biweekly report for current/past periods
Monthly report generation works with period_index
Yearly report generation works
Custom date range report works with start_date and end_date
Custom range requires both dates (400 if missing)
Hourly rate override works correctly
Employee filter works correctly
Invalid period_type rejected with 400
Report includes: period info, settings, summary (employees/hours/shifts/wages), employee breakdown with shifts
POST /api/admin/payroll/report/pdf generates valid PDF
PDF has correct content-type (application/pdf)
PDF has content-disposition with filename
PDF starts with %PDF header
Biweekly, monthly, yearly, and custom range PDFs all work
PDF with hourly rate override works
Payroll button visible in admin dashboard header (gold-colored)
Payroll Reports modal opens with period type selector
Period type options: Biweekly, Monthly, Yearly, Custom Range
Period selector shows Current/Last/Previous periods
Hourly Rate input visible and editable
Employee filter dropdown works
Generate Report button creates on-screen report
Report shows summary cards (Employees, Total Hours, Total Shifts, Total Wages)
Employee breakdown table shows hours, shifts, rate, and gross wages
Download PDF button appears after report generation
Settings button visible in payroll modal
Settings modal opens with Pay Period Start Date and Default Hourly Rate inputs
Save Settings button saves configuration

passed_tests: Custom range requires start_date and end_date (400)
PDF with hourly rate override works
Frontend: Generate Report creates on-screen report
Frontend: Employee breakdown with hours, shifts, rate, wages
Frontend: Download PDF button visible after report
All 53 previous tests still pass (1 pre-existing flaky test)
critical_code_review_comments: Minor: Console hydration warnings about <tr> inside <span> persist - likely React rendering wrapper issue in table body (non-blocking)
seed_data_creation: Backend tests create test employees with unique UUIDs (payroll_test_*, payroll_report_test_*, payroll_pdf_emp_*)
context_for_next_testing_agent: Payroll period tracking feature fully tested and working. All 21 new payroll tests pass. Total 74 backend tests. PDF generation confirmed working (PDF starts with %PDF header). Console hydration warnings persist from previous iteration.

mocked_api.Resend email API: Using placeholder key re_123_placeholder - emails skipped in notification flow
